---
title: "02_Descriptive"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE) 
```

```{r}
############################################################
# 0. Load Packages
############################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom.mixed)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
############################################################
# 1. Load analytical dataset
############################################################

# The final fake-headline sample is created at the end of
# the data preparation script and loaded here for descriptive checks.

df_fake <- read_csv("data_processed/df_fake.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure correct variable types
df_fake <- df_fake %>%
  mutate(
    study_id        = factor(study_id),
    item_id         = factor(item_id),
    participant_id  = factor(participant_id),
    experiment_type = factor(experiment_type),
    gender          = factor(gender),
    ideology        = factor(ideology),
    veracity        = factor(veracity),
    share_z         = as.numeric(share_z),
    share_intent    = as.numeric(share_intent),
    age             = as.numeric(age),
    age_z           = as.numeric(age_z)
  )

# Optional overall dimensions
dim(df_fake)
head(df_fake)
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 2. General sample overview
############################################################

df_fake %>%
  summarise(
    observations = n(),
    participants = n_distinct(participant_id),
    headlines    = n_distinct(item_id),
    studies      = n_distinct(study_id)
  )

df_fake %>%
  count(experiment_type)

df_fake %>%
  summarise(
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age   = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1       = quantile(age, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3       = quantile(age, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_age  = min(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_age  = max(age, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

```

```{r}
############################################################
# 3. Missing values
############################################################

df_fake %>%
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    na_share  = sum(is.na(share_z)),
    na_age    = sum(is.na(age)),
    na_ideo   = sum(is.na(ideology)),
    na_gender = sum(is.na(gender)),
    na_verac  = sum(is.na(veracity))
  )


```


```{r}
############################################################
# 4. Distribution of key variables
############################################################

# 4.1 Distribution of standardized sharing intentions
summary(df_fake$share_z)

ggplot(df_fake, aes(x = share_z, fill = experiment_type)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, bins = 30, position = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of standardized sharing intentions (share_z)",
    x = "share_z",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

# 4.2 Age distribution
summary(df_fake$age)

ggplot(df_fake, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Age distribution",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Frequency"
  )
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 5. Sample composition by experiment type
############################################################

# 5.1 Number of observations by experiment type
df_fake %>%
  count(experiment_type)

# 5.2 Number of unique participants by experiment type
df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type) %>%
  summarise(
    participants = n_distinct(participant_id),
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 5.3 Number of unique headlines by study and experiment type
df_fake %>%
  group_by(study_id) %>%
  summarise(unique_headlines = n_distinct(item_id), .groups = "drop")

df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type) %>%
  summarise(unique_headlines = n_distinct(item_id), .groups = "drop")
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 6. Descriptive statistics by experiment type
############################################################

desc_fake_exp <- df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_share = mean(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_share   = sd(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    n          = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

desc_fake_exp


```




```{r}
############################################################
# 7. Demographic composition
############################################################

# 7.1 Ideology distribution
df_fake %>%
  count(ideology)

df_fake %>%
  count(experiment_type, ideology)

# 7.2 Gender distribution
df_fake %>%
  count(gender)

df_fake %>%
  count(experiment_type, gender)

# 7.3 Age by experiment type
df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age   = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```



```{r}
############################################################
# 8. Exploratory descriptive patterns
############################################################

# 8.1 share_z by experiment type and ideology
df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type, ideology) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_share = mean(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_share   = sd(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    n          = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 8.2 Age groups (tertiles) by experiment type
df_fake <- df_fake %>%
  mutate(age_group = ntile(age, 3))

df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type, age_group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_share = mean(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_share   = sd(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    n          = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 8.3 Plot: mean share_z with 95% CI by experiment type
df_fake %>%
  group_by(experiment_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_share = mean(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_share   = sd(share_z, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = experiment_type, y = mean_share)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_share - 1.96 * se_share,
      ymax = mean_share + 1.96 * se_share
    ),
    width = 0.1
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean share_z (false headlines) by experiment type",
    x = "Experiment type",
    y = "Mean share_z"
  )

```


```{r}
############################################################
# 9. Intervention coding checks
############################################################

# These checks verify whether treatment indicators vary where expected.

# 9.1 Accuracy nudge indicator
table(df_fake$experiment_type, df_fake$accuracy_nudge)

# 9.2 Source-rating indicator
table(df_fake$experiment_type, df_fake$rating_shown)

# 9.3 Fact-check-label indicator
table(df_fake$experiment_type, df_fake$label_shown)


```


```{r}
############################################################
# 10. Simple plausibility checks
############################################################

# 10.1 Correlation between share_z and age
cor(df_fake$share_z, df_fake$age, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# 10.2 Mean share_z by ideology
df_fake %>%
  group_by(ideology) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_share = mean(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_share   = sd(share_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    n          = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 11. Additional plots for age-standardized distributions
############################################################

ggplot(df_fake, aes(x = age_z, fill = factor(accuracy_nudge))) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", bins = 30) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of age_z by accuracy nudge status",
    fill = "Accuracy nudge"
  )

ggplot(df_fake, aes(x = age_z, fill = factor(label_shown))) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", bins = 30) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of age_z by fact-check label status",
    fill = "Label shown"
  )

ggplot(df_fake, aes(x = age_z, fill = factor(rating_shown))) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", bins = 30) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of age_z by source-rating status",
    fill = "Rating shown"
  )
```
